{"id":"project-LICHEN-nqz6","title":"Spec: Simplify addressing to link-local + Yggdrasil only","description":"## Overview

Update spec to reflect simplified addressing model: drop ULA, use Yggdrasil-derived 02xx addresses as the primary unicast address.

## Files to update

### spec/04-network.md (or addressing section)
- Remove ULA (fd00:lichen::/32) references
- Document 02xx as primary address
- Explain: works locally without Yggdrasil, works globally with Yggdrasil

### spec/05-routing.md
- Update routing decision logic
- 02xx destinations: check local mesh first, then Yggdrasil
- Remove ULA routing rules

### spec/01-architecture.md
- Update addressing overview
- Two addresses: link-local + 02xx (not three)

### spec/06-security.md
- Document Ed25519 → Yggdrasil address derivation
- Same key for: signatures, OSCORE, IID, Yggdrasil address

## New content

### Address derivation
```
Ed25519 public key (32 bytes)
        ↓
    SHA-512 hash (64 bytes)
        ↓
    Truncate + 02 prefix
        ↓
02xx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx
```

### Address scopes
| Address | Scope | Use |
|---------|-------|-----|
| fe80::<iid> | Link-local | NDP, RPL control, neighbor discovery |
| 02xx::<iid> | Global | All application traffic, everywhere |

### Why no ULA
- ULA was for "mesh-internal without gateway"
- 02xx works locally too — doesn't require Yggdrasil daemon
- Simpler: one address for everything
- No "which address for this destination" confusion","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-16T20:55:02.925597Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-16T20:55:02.925597Z","labels":["spec","addressing","yggdrasil"]}

```
Ed25519 public key (32 bytes)

02xx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx
```

### Address scopes

### Why no ULA
- ULA was for "mesh-internal without gateway"
- 02xx works locally too — doesn't require Yggdrasil daemon
- Simpler: one address for everything
- No "which address for this destination" confusion","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"Mark Atwood","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-16T20:55:02.925597Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-20T05:44:53.664069Z","started_at":"2026-07-20T05:44:53.664069Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-20T05:49:53.664069Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-20T05:44:53.664069Z","labels":["spec","addressing","yggdrasil"]}
new_value: {"acceptance_criteria":"Normative architecture, network, routing, security, SCHC, RPL, LCI, packet examples, and application examples consistently specify link-local control plus native per-node Yggdrasil /128 application addressing; no baseline ULA remains; exact AddrForKey algorithm and an upstream-derived test vector are included; global handoff limitation is explicit; documentation checks and three independent reviews pass.","design":"Use current upstream yggdrasil-go AddrForKey compatibility, not the issue's obsolete SHA-512 sketch. Native addresses are 0200::/8 per-node /128s derived directly from the raw Ed25519 public key. Link-local IID remains SHA-256(pubkey)[0:8] with the U/L bit cleared, matching current Python and Zephyr implementations. ULA and baseline GUA are removed. Local full-/128 routes win; unknown native destinations use an operational identity-preserving Yggdrasil handoff or fail unreachable. An ordinary gateway-owned Yggdrasil daemon cannot impersonate node-native sources; 0300::/8 gateway subnets are rejected because they violate stable per-node addressing."}

new_value: {"description":"Update all normative specs and supporting documentation to use link-local control addresses plus per-node native Yggdrasil 0200::/8 /128 application addresses. Remove ULA and baseline GUA assignment. Define current upstream AddrForKey exactly, local-only baseline routing, realm-local multicast, SCHC rule-set v2, trust binding, collision handling, and independent vectors."}

Remaining blockers: distinct RPL Instance IDs and authenticated sender realm membership; relay algorithm enforcement of origin/immediate-sender/realm aggregate budgets; ambiguous short SenderID must force extended form; scoped link-local examples; stale non-SOS multicast examples; root-DIO key bootstrap; mandatory Announce replay persistence; exact DIO base layout; singleton realm option; RFC 8949 SOS deterministic encoding profile; monotonic trust-anchor generation; explicit OSCORE peer-key/native-source binding; exact group mutation signatures/replay and mandatory rekey after removal; rekey before 24-bit link counter reuse. python/tests/test_lci_contract_docs.py intentionally fails on the old direct-routing assertion; project-LICHEN-jtup owns runtime/test alignment. Do not begin affected implementation lanes until this issue closes."}
